project_overview.project_name: Economic Management and Statistics Development for Policy Making Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen the capacity of the National Institute of Statistics of Djibouti (INSD) to produce and disseminate timely and reliable statistics in a sustainable manner and modernize the Recipient's selected economic and fiscal management tools and processes in Djibouti.
project_overview.total_financing: US$17.0 million (IDA Credit: US$15.0 million, Government counterpart: US$2.0 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Citizens of Djibouti (ultimate beneficiaries), technical institutions of central government, Central Bank of Djibouti, INSD, Ministry of Finance departments, Ministry of Budget departments

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Djibouti is politically stable but surrounded by areas in conflict or fragile situations, hosts 30,000 refugees and asylum seekers from Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Yemen, plus 150,000 non-registered migrants

key_components.component_name: Strengthening the capacity of the National Institute of Statistics of Djibouti (INSD)
key_components.description: Setting up INSD infrastructure, building capacity, conducting foundational data collection including Population Census, Economic Census, household surveys, and improving statistical production and dissemination

key_components.component_name: Modernizing selected economic and fiscal management tools and processes
key_components.description: Building macroeconomic modeling capacity, developing Medium-Term Budget Framework, improving investment planning, and strengthening debt management
Establishing and operating Project Management Unit, coordination, fiduciary management, monitoring and reporting

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Economy and Finance in charge of Industry (primary), with support from Ministry of Budget and National Institute of Statistics of Djibouti
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Management Unit (PMU) within Ministry of Finance, Project Steering Committee co-chaired by Ministers of Finance and Budget, implementation agreements with INSD
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers, international competitive bidding for major contracts, national procedures for smaller contracts
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PMU responsible for monitoring, quarterly IFRs, annual Project Financial Statements, external audit, results framework tracking

identified_risks.risk_description: Erosion of public trust in state institutions could undermine government's ability to implement Vision 2035
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Policy dialogue, systematic citizen engagement interventions, leveraging broad consensus around Vision 2035

identified_risks.risk_description: High vulnerability to exogenous shocks, high public debt (71% of GDP), small economy size
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Prudent fiscal policy and debt management, project activities to strengthen macroeconomic policy capacity

identified_risks.risk_description: Low capacity and lack of coordination across public sector entities, weak statistical capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong government commitment, upstream engagement, Delivery Unit creation, capacity building activities

identified_risks.risk_description: Very weak fiduciary environment, limited oversight institutions, weak internal controls
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: PMU establishment, external auditor recruitment, accounting software acquisition, Project Operations Manual

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level grievance mechanism established, World Bank Grievance Redress Service available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender quotas for staff recruitment, 50% women participation target in training, production of sex-disaggregated statistics, UNSD Minimum List gender indicators

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed considering Djibouti's location surrounded by conflict-affected areas, includes activities to strengthen state capacity and evidence-based policy making to support stability
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Small state context, high debt levels, vulnerability to external shocks, weak institutional capacity, surrounded by fragile/conflict-affected neighbors
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Presence of five military bases with 10,000 staff, road safety concerns during census activities, security risks from regional instability
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - hosts approximately 30,000 registered refugees and asylum seekers from Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Yemen, plus 150,000 non-registered migrants; country serves as transit point for economic migrants and forcibly displaced populations
